chunks.en: I usually need to print meeting notes and some contracts every week.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい毎週、会議のメモと契約書を印刷する必要があります。

chunks.en: When the printer runs out of paper, I open the tray and add more.
chunks.ja: プリンターの紙が切れたときは、トレイを開けて紙を足します。

chunks.en: Sometimes I wait in line because a coworker is printing a big file.
chunks.ja: 同僚が大きなファイルを印刷しているので、順番待ちをすることもあります。

chunks.en: Last week the printer had a paper jam, so I pulled the paper out slowly.
chunks.ja: 先週プリンターが紙詰まりを起こしたので、私はゆっくり紙を引き抜きました。

chunks.en: After the pages come out, I grab them and check they are all there.
chunks.ja: ページが出てきたら、それを取って全部そろっているか確認します。

chunks.en: Then I take my pages back to my desk and start working again.
chunks.ja: それから印刷物を持って席に戻り、また作業を始めます。

outline: Opening: what I print at work
Point: dealing with empty paper and jams
Point: waiting and picking up pages

modelTalk.text: I don't print much, but sometimes I really need to. I usually print emails, meeting notes, or a report for my boss. When I click print, I walk to the machine and wait. Sometimes the printer runs out of paper. So I open the tray and put more paper in. It's easy, and I feel happy when it works. But some days there's a long line of people. I wait for my turn, and I talk with a friend. One time the paper got stuck inside. I opened the door and pulled it out slowly. After that, my pages came out warm. I pick them up and check the order. Then I put them together and walk back to my desk. It's a small thing, but it feels good. I like a clean, tidy desk.

chunks.en: Today I'll talk about using the office printer, which I do almost every day.
chunks.ja: 今日はオフィスのプリンターを使うことについて話します。ほぼ毎日やっています。

chunks.en: I usually need to print meeting notes and system design documents for my team.
chunks.ja: 私はいつもチームのために会議メモやシステム設計書を印刷する必要があります。

chunks.en: When the printer runs out of paper, I open the tray and add a new stack.
chunks.ja: プリンターの紙が切れたときは、トレイを開けて新しい紙の束を足します。

chunks.en: One time I had to wait for a paper jam, so I fixed it myself.
chunks.ja: あるとき紙詰まりで待たされたので、自分で直しました。

chunks.en: After the pages come out, I grab them and walk back to my desk.
chunks.ja: ページが出てきたら、それを取って自分の席に戻ります。

outline: Opening: I use the office printer daily
Point 1: What I print and handling out-of-paper
Point 2: Waiting and fixing a paper jam
Wrap-up: Grabbing pages and returning to my desk

modelTalk.text: I print stuff at work almost every day. Usually it's reports, meeting notes, or a form I need to sign. Sometimes the printer runs out of paper. When that happens, I open the tray and add more paper. It's easy, so I don't panic. But sometimes other people are printing too. So I have to wait my turn near the machine. Once there was a paper jam, and that was annoying. I opened the little door and pulled the paper out slowly. Then it worked again. When my pages come out, I check they're all mine. I don't want to grab someone else's copies. I pick up my sheets, staple them if I need to, and walk back to my desk. Then I sit down and get back to work. It's a small thing, but I do it a lot.
